title: senecio farinaceus
intro: Senecio farinaceus, also known as the mealy ragwort, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and relieve respiratory issues. The health benefits of Senecio farinaceus include reducing inflammation, calming the nervous system, and acting as a natural expectorant to help clear mucus from the lungs. Herbal preparations of Senecio farinaceus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat colds, coughs, and bronchitis.
